{
  "cells": [
    {
      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "source": [
        "# Multi-Feature Anomaly Detection in Transactional Systems  \n",
        "### A Machine Learning Approach Using Behavioral Data"
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "id": "hqXCo0xaLoL6"
      }
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "source": [
        "## 1. Problem Statement\n",
        "\n",
        "Modern transactional and distributed systems often contain hidden anomalies that signal fraud, inefficiencies, or abnormal behavior.\n",
        "\n",
        "Detecting these anomalies is critical for maintaining system integrity, optimizing performance, and ensuring trust. This project explores how machine learning can identify such irregularities using structured, multi-dimensional behavioral data."
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "id": "cZ_K1kD3MnsV"
      }
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "source": [
        "## 2. Objective\n",
        "\n",
        "The objective of this project is to design a simple yet effective anomaly detection system that leverages multiple behavioral features to identify unusual patterns in transaction-like data."
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "id": "ZAXp8CulNABo"
      }
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "source": [
        "## 3. Approach\n",
        "\n",
        "This project follows a structured approach:\n",
        "\n",
        "- Simulate transaction-like data with multiple behavioral features  \n",
        "- Introduce controlled anomalies to mimic abnormal patterns  \n",
        "- Apply an unsupervised machine learning model  \n",
        "- Analyze and visualize detected anomalies"
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "id": "SLMAbfohNF-h"
      }
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "source": [
        "import pandas as pd\n",
        "import numpy as np\n",
        "import matplotlib.pyplot as plt\n",
        "from sklearn.ensemble import IsolationForest"
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "id": "aYtN4TmjOdE3"
      },
      "execution_count": null,
      "outputs": []
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "source": [
        "## 4. Data Description\n",
        "\n",
        "The dataset simulates transactional behavior using three key features:\n",
        "\n",
        "- **Amount**: Represents transaction value  \n",
        "- **Frequency**: Indicates how often transactions occur  \n",
        "- **Time Gap**: Measures intervals between transactions  \n",
        "\n",
        "Synthetic anomalies are introduced to represent irregular or suspicious behavior."
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "id": "CKizUI_GN_6A"
      }
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "source": [
        "import pandas as pd\n",
        "import numpy as np\n",
        "\n",
        "# Set random seed for reproducibility\n",
        "np.random.seed(42)\n",
        "\n",
        "# Create normal data\n",
        "df_normal = pd.DataFrame({\n",
        "    'amount': np.random.normal(100, 15, 100),\n",
        "    'frequency': np.random.normal(10, 2, 100),\n",
        "    'time_gap': np.random.normal(5, 1, 100)\n",
        "})\n",
        "\n",
        "# Create anomaly data\n",
        "df_anomaly = pd.DataFrame({\n",
        "    'amount': np.random.normal(300, 50, 10),\n",
        "    'frequency': np.random.normal(2, 1, 10),\n",
        "    'time_gap': np.random.normal(20, 5, 10)\n",
        "})\n",
        "\n",
        "# Combine both DataFrames\n",
        "df = pd.concat([df_normal, df_anomaly], ignore_index=True)\n",
        "\n",
        "# Display the first 5 rows\n",
        "df.head()"
      ],

    {
      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "source": [
        "## 5. Model Explanation\n",
        "\n",
        "This project uses the Isolation Forest algorithm for anomaly detection.\n",
        "\n",
        "Isolation Forest works by isolating rare observations rather than modeling normal behavior directly.\n",
        "\n",
        "- Output:\n",
        "  - **1** → Normal observation  \n",
        "    - **-1** → Anomalous observation  \n",
        "\n",
        "    The **contamination parameter** defines the expected proportion of anomalies and helps calibrate the model’s sensitivity."
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "id": "YSQAraXraHhK"
      }
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "metadata": {
        "id": "174aa91b"
      },
      "source": [
        "### 5.1. Feature Scaling (Improvement Suggestion)\n",
        "\n",
        "Before applying the Isolation Forest, it's often beneficial to scale features, especially if they have vastly different ranges or units. This helps ensure that no single feature dominates the anomaly detection process due to its magnitude. We'll use `StandardScaler` to transform the features to have a mean of 0 and a standard deviation of 1."
      ]
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "metadata": {
        "colab": {
          "base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/",
          "height": 223
        },
        "id": "5a97d988",
        "outputId": "eee0e809-db69-44c5-cd28-d2141b48e58a"
      },
      "source": [
        "from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler\n",
        "\n",
        "# Select the features for scaling\n",
        "features = ['amount', 'frequency', 'time_gap']\n",
        "\n",
        "# Initialize the StandardScaler\n",
        "scaler = StandardScaler()\n",
        "\n",
        "# Fit the scaler and transform the features\n",
        "df_scaled_features = pd.DataFrame(scaler.fit_transform(df[features]), columns=features)\n",
        "\n",
        "# Display the first few rows of the scaled features\n",
        "print(\"Scaled Features Head:\")\n",
        "display(df_scaled_features.head())\n"
      ],
      "execution_count": 19,
      "outputs": [
        {
          "output_type": "stream",
          "name": "stdout",
          "text": [
            "Scaled Features Head:\n"
          ]
        },

    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "source": [
        "from sklearn.ensemble import IsolationForest\n",
        "\n",
        "model = IsolationForest(contamination=0.1, random_state=42)\n",
        "df['anomaly'] = model.fit_predict(df[['amount', 'frequency', 'time_gap']])\n",
        "\n",
        "df.head()"
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "colab": {
          "base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/",
          "height": 206
        },
        "id": "bskf9492dhMF",
        "outputId": "e5860cf3-54cd-4624-f30f-4615c39b8506"
      },
      "execution_count": 15,
      "outputs": [

      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "metadata": {
        "id": "a04b7dd1"
      },
      "source": [
        "### 5.2. Consideration: Contamination Parameter (Further Thought)\n",
        "\n",
        "In our synthetic data, we knew the approximate number of anomalies. However, in a real-world scenario with unlabeled data, how would you determine a reasonable value for the `contamination` parameter? What are the implications of setting it too high or too low, and how might you approach estimating it if you had no prior knowledge?"
      ]
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "metadata": {
        "id": "b0ebfc74"
      },
      "source": [
        "### 5.3. Consideration: Quantitative Evaluation (Further Thought)\n",
        "\n",
        "Since this is an unsupervised method, evaluating performance can be tricky without ground truth. If you *did* have a small set of labeled anomalies, what quantitative metrics would you use to assess the effectiveness of your Isolation Forest model (e.g., Precision, Recall, F1-score)? How might you calculate them, and what would each metric tell you about the model's performance?"
      ]
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "source": [
        "## 6. Visualization\n",
        "\n",
        "To better understand the model’s behavior, we visualize how anomalies are distributed across key features."
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "id": "Fvqz5FK8eTgx"
      }
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "source": [
        "import matplotlib.pyplot as plt\n",
        "\n",
        "plt.figure(figsize=(8,5))\n",
        "plt.scatter(df['amount'], df['frequency'], c=df['anomaly'])\n",
        "plt.xlabel(\"Transaction Amount\")\n",
        "plt.ylabel(\"Transaction Frequency\")\n",
        "plt.title(\"Anomaly Detection Based on Behavioral Patterns\")\n",
        "plt.show()"
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "colab": {
          "base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/",
          "height": 324
        },
        "id": "stEXBAjEexDu",
        "outputId": "794118ed-b1c8-4c81-8c31-54feb28503d7"
      },
      "execution_count": 17,
      "outputs": [
        {
          "output_type": "display_data",
          "data": {
            "text/plain": [
              "<Figure size 800x500 with 1 Axes>"
            ],
            "image/png": "\n"
          },
          "metadata": {}
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "metadata": {
        "id": "e2a8fc8c"
      },
      "source": [
        "### 6.1. 3D Visualization of Anomalies (Improvement Suggestion)\n",
        "\n",
        "To visualize anomalies across all three features, a 3D scatter plot can be very insightful. This allows us to see how `amount`, `frequency`, and `time_gap` interact to define both normal and anomalous behavior."
      ]
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "metadata": {
        "colab": {
          "base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/",
          "height": 476
        },
        "id": "1338f120",
        "outputId": "cae80e92-9f8f-4224-d5c6-fc02d7241e03"
      },
      "source": [
        "import matplotlib.pyplot as plt\n",
        "from mpl_toolkits.mplot3d import Axes3D\n",
        "\n",
        "fig = plt.figure(figsize=(10, 8))\n",
        "ax = fig.add_subplot(111, projection='3d')\n",
        "\n",
        "# Separate normal and anomalous points for clearer plotting\n",
        "normal_points = df[df['anomaly'] == 1]\n",
        "anomaly_points = df[df['anomaly'] == -1]\n",
        "\n",
        "# Plot normal points in one color\n",
        "ax.scatter(normal_points['amount'], normal_points['frequency'], normal_points['time_gap'],\n",
        "           c='blue', label='Normal', s=20)\n",
        "\n",
        "# Plot anomaly points in a different color\n",
        "ax.scatter(anomaly_points['amount'], anomaly_points['frequency'], anomaly_points['time_gap'],\n",
        "           c='red', label='Anomaly', s=50, marker='X')\n",
        "\n",
        "ax.set_xlabel('Transaction Amount')\n",
        "ax.set_ylabel('Transaction Frequency')\n",
        "ax.set_zlabel('Time Gap')\n",
        "ax.set_title('3D Anomaly Detection Based on Behavioral Patterns')\n",
        "ax.legend()\n",
        "plt.show()\n"
      ],
      "execution_count": 20,
      "outputs": [
        {
          "output_type": "display_data",
          "data": {
            "text/plain": [
              "<Figure size 1000x800 with 1 Axes>"
            ],
            "image/png": "\n"
          },
          "metadata": {}
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "source": [
        "## 7. Results Interpretation\n",
        "\n",
        "The model successfully identifies anomalous patterns across multiple features.\n",
        "\n",
        "Unlike single-variable detection methods, this approach captures complex relationships between behavioral indicators, making it more reflective of real-world system dynamics."
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "id": "fcerzW6zfUAS"
      }
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "source": [
        "anomalies = df[df['anomaly'] == -1]\n",

        "print(\"Detected anomalies:\", len(anomalies))\n",
        "\n",
        "anomalies.head()"
